course.name: FREE COURSE TEST - Price Zero Validation
course.description: This course tests that price: 0 (FREE courses) work correctly without validation errors.

modules.title: Module 1: Testing Price Zero
modules.description: This module verifies that free courses (price: 0) import successfully.
modules.content:
Testing Price: 0

If you can see this module, it means the validation correctly accepts price: 0 for FREE courses!

What We're Testing:

  • ✅ Price field exists
  • ✅ Price is exactly 0 (zero)
  • ✅ Validation should accept it
  • ✅ Course should be created successfully

Validation Logic:

if (course.price === undefined || course.price === null) {
  throw error
}
// This correctly accepts: price: 0, 1, 100, etc.
// This correctly rejects: price: undefined, null, missing

questions.question: What is the price of this course?
questions.explanation: This course has price: 0, which makes it completely FREE. The validation should accept 0 as a valid price value.

questions.question: True or False: The validation code 'if (!course.price)' would reject price: 0
questions.explanation: TRUE! The code 'if (!course.price)' treats 0 as falsy and would incorrectly reject it. That's why we use 'if (course.price === undefined || course.price === null)' instead, which correctly accepts 0.

questions.question: Which validation check correctly accepts price: 0?
questions.explanation: Option 2 is correct! It explicitly checks for undefined or null, allowing 0 as a valid value. Option 1 would reject 0 (falsy), Option 3 has confusing logic, and Option 4 is incorrect.
